sessions.summary: Published /analyze skill to private GitHub repo for friend handoff
accomplishments: Created private GitHub repo at github.com/DubiWork/Video-Analyzer and pushed local skill (no prior remote, was local-only since v0.1.0 ship)
Switched origin from HTTPS to SSH (git@github.com:DubiWork/Video-Analyzer.git) to use existing DubiWork keyring login — avoided needing the new PAT
Discovered GITHUB_TOKEN env var holds an invalid token; gh keyring + SSH still works when env var is unset per-command — saved as memory `reference-gh-cli-keyring-vs-env-token`
Token leaked once into Claude conversation, rotated immediately at github.com/settings/tokens
Documented friend setup steps: clone repo, pip install requirements, install ffmpeg+yt-dlp, configure ~/.config/video-analyzer/.env with their LiteLLM proxy URL/key/model
nextSteps: Get friend's GitHub username and run `unset GITHUB_TOKEN && gh api -X PUT repos/DubiWork/Video-Analyzer/collaborators/<username> -f permission=pull` to send the read-only collaborator invite
Optional cleanup: find where GITHUB_TOKEN env var is set (Windows User Environment Variables or shell rc) and remove the invalid value
